text:
Once upon a time, in the quiet town of Serenity Springs, there was a peculiar old house shrouded in mystery, nestled at the edge of a whispering forest. Within its walls lived the quirky and retired librarian, Miss Beatrice Whittaker, who knew every nook and cranny by heart.

Each morning, under the serene gaze of the rising sun, Miss Whittaker would dust away the cobwebs and lend her unique perspective on old book tales to whoever cared to sit in her cozy reading nook. Her stories were rich tales of fantasy and bravery, tinged with a hint of her personal wisdom about life’s minor annoyances.

One drizzly evening, a young boy named Ethan wobbled up, his shoes squelching in the wet grass. He was on his way to visit Miss Whittaker; he had heard her stories so many times, yet they still managed to stir something deep inside. Today, he brought an old, dog-eared picturebook
